# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build & Notification with Details
on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  build_and_notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      pull-requests: read # Required to read PR title and body
      
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Extract PR details
      id: pr_details
      run: |
        PR_TITLE="${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}"
        PR_DESCRIPTION="${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}"
        PR_URL="${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}"

        echo "pr_title=$PR_TITLE" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "pr_description=$PR_DESCRIPTION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "pr_url=$PR_URL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        
        echo "--- Extracted PR Details ---"
        echo "Title: $PR_TITLE"
        echo "Description: $PR_DESCRIPTION"
        echo "URL: $PR_URL"
        echo "--------------------------"

    - name: Simulate build step
      run: |
        echo "Starting build for feature: ${{ steps.pr_details.outputs.pr_title }}"
        # Your actual build commands would go here, e.g.:
        # npm ci
        # npm run build
        echo "Build artifacts generated."

    - name: Simulate commit message for release automation (using PR title)
      run: |
        echo "This step demonstrates how you'd use the PR title in a commit message for a release."
        echo "For example, when creating a release commit or a tag message:"
        echo "  git commit -m \"Release feature: ${{ steps.pr_details.outputs.pr_title }}\""
        echo "  git tag -a v1.0.0 -m \"Release of feature: ${{ steps.pr_details.outputs.pr_title }}\""
        echo ""
        echo "Actual commit/tag creation would require additional git configuration and 'contents: write' permission."

    - name: Simulate sending build notification
      env:
        PR_TITLE: ${{ steps.pr_details.outputs.pr_title }}
        PR_DESCRIPTION: ${{ steps.pr_details.outputs.pr_description }}
        PR_URL: ${{ steps.pr_details.outputs.pr_url }}
        # SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }} # Uncomment and set as a secret for real notifications
      run: |
        echo "--- Simulating Notification ---"
        echo "Sending notification about feature deployment..."
        
        NOTIFICATION_MESSAGE="
        *Feature Deployment Notification*
        ------------------------------------
        *Feature:* $PR_TITLE
        *Description:*
        $PR_DESCRIPTION
        *PR Link:* $PR_URL
        *Status:* Build Successful 🎉
        ------------------------------------
        "
        echo "$NOTIFICATION_MESSAGE"
        
        # Example of sending to Slack (requires SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL secret)
        # curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' --data "{\"text\": \"$NOTIFICATION_MESSAGE\"}" $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL
        
        echo "Notification content generated successfully."
